description_long: This is a modest, single-story wooden house with a dark, weathered exterior and a steep gable roof. The facade features five rectangular windows with blue trim, arranged in a symmetrical row, and a small attic vent above the central window. The roof is covered in corrugated material, showing signs of age and some discoloration, and includes a single metal chimney pipe. A simple, brown wooden gate is attached to one side of the house, suggesting a fenced yard or entryway. The house sits low to the ground, with no visible steps or porch, and the overall design is utilitarian and unadorned. The structure evokes a traditional rural or vernacular architectural style, commonly found in Eastern European or Russian settings.
description: A small, weathered wooden house with a steep gable roof, blue-trimmed windows, and a simple side gate. The design is symmetrical and utilitarian, with a rustic, rural character.
description_view_0: Front view showing five blue-trimmed windows, a small attic vent, and the attached brown gate on the left.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the long, corrugated roof, the row of windows, and the brown gate extending outward.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, with the same window and attic vent arrangement but no visible gate.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with the brown gate now on the right, showing the full length of the house and roof.
attribute_entryway_design: Simple door with no porch or steps, adjacent to a plain wooden gate, lacking decorative elements
